source_jobs.title: Head of Field Engineering
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Head of Field Engineering, you'll build and lead the team responsible for the technical side of Runlayer's customer motion. You'll own Forward Deployed Engineering end-to-end, taking the function from three engineers today to ten by year-end and into a multi-region organization in the year that follows.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: You will define how Runlayer's customers move from initial interest to enterprise-wide deployment, owning the team that converts pilots into production at the world's most demanding companies.

 - Excellence: You'll partner directly with the CEO, CPO, and VP of Go-to-Market to build the technical playbook that takes a customer from first touch to thousands of seats deployed.

 - Ownership: You'll recruit, hire, ramp, and lead the team; build the onboarding program; codify operational standards; and establish the technical best practices that scale with the business.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and lead the Forward Deployed Engineering team. Recruit, hire, and onboard the next seven Forward Deployed Engineers, with continued hiring through the following year.

 - Establish the onboarding program. Define how new Forward Deployed Engineers ramp on the product, demo motion, pilot execution, and customer engagement standards.

 - Bring operational rigor to the existing team. Codify how the team moves a customer from initial interest to a thousand-plus seat deployment, with consistent quality and predictable outcomes.

 - Define the technical playbook for enterprise deployments. Develop the practices that govern how Runlayer is integrated, configured, and scaled inside large enterprise environments.

 - Partner across the leadership team. Work closely with Go-to-Market, Product, and Engineering leadership on team design, compensation structure, territory model, and customer health.

 - Scale the function programmatically. In year two, hire regional leaders, operate through them, and stand up a repeatable execution layer underneath.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Senior operating experience. Ten or more years of relevant professional experience, including significant time in senior individual contributor and leadership roles within technical, customer-facing organizations.

 - Strong technical foundation. A degree in Computer Science or a closely related technical field from a strong undergraduate program.

 - Practitioner background. Direct experience as a Forward Deployed Engineer, Solutions Engineer, or in a comparable technical customer-facing capacity. You've done the work yourself before leading others doing it.

 - Early-stage operating experience. Time spent at an early-stage startup, where you've built a function or team from the ground up.

 - Experience at a category leader. Time at an established, top-tier technology company (such as Databricks, Snowflake, Palantir, Salesforce, or similar) whe

source_jobs.title: Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As the founding Support Engineer at Runlayer, you'll be the technical frontline for enterprise customers deploying Runlayer across their engineering orgs. You'll work directly with customers in Pylon, Slack, and Teams to triage and resolve issues across the control plane, connectors, OAuth flows, deployment configs, and AI client integrations, and you'll build the support playbook from scratch as the bridge between customers and engineering.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Be the first support hire and the face of Runlayer to every enterprise customer in production.

 - Excellence: Work closely with the engineers who built the platform, and use AI tooling daily to do the job.

 - Ownership: Own the support function end to end, from triage and runbooks to the fixes that kill recurring tickets.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Work directly with customers in Pylon, Slack, and Teams to triage and resolve technical issues and bugs across the control plane, MCP connectors, OAuth, and deployments.

 - Use AI and MCP tooling to triage faster: diagnostic agents, log query templates, and reproduction workflows.

 - Guide enterprise teams through onboarding: first setup, SSO and SCIM, connector activation, and policy configuration.

 - Build the knowledge base from zero: runbooks, troubleshooting guides, and customer-facing docs for common deployment patterns (EKS, ECS, Helm, Terraform).

 - Manage escalations: debug what you can, and pull in Platform, SRE, or Identity engineers with clear reproduction steps when you can't.

 - Close the loop with product and engineering: turn recurring tickets into doc fixes, product fixes, and self-serve flows.

 - Learn and share new features as they ship each week, both to customers and across the team.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3-5 years in technical support, solutions engineering, or customer-facing engineering at SaaS or developer-tools companies.

 - An infrastructure support background, or strong technical depth in enterprise infra and AI systems like agents and identity.

 - Debugging fluency across the stack: you read Python/TypeScript, query PostgreSQL, trace HTTP requests, and interpret Kubernetes pod logs.

 - Familiarity with enterprise identity (SSO, SCIM, OAuth) and cloud infrastructure (AWS preferred: EKS/ECS, CloudWatch, VPCs).

 - Experience running support in Pylon and over Slack or Teams.

 - AI-native workflow: you use MCPs and AI tools daily to do real work

 - A drive to put customer satisfaction first and go above and beyond.

 - Excellent written communication: clear, concise explanations for both engineers and IT admins.

 - Zero-to-one mindset: comfortable defining your own SLAs, triage workflows, and escalation paths.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Hands-on experience with MCP and how agents call tools in production.

 - Experience supporting security-critical or compliance-driven enterpris

source_jobs.title: Demand Generation Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Demand Gen Lead, you'll build Runlayer's paid acquisition engine from zero. We run no paid today (all inbound is organic), but we have the budget, the deal sizes, and the pipeline pressure to make paid a real channel. You'll own paid ads, landing pages, SEO/AEO, and growth experimentation.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Build the paid acquisition motion from scratch for the enterprise platform to run AI faster & safely

 - Excellence: Work alongside founders and a senior GTM team closing Fortune 500 deals through pure inbound

 - Ownership: Own demand gen end-to-end, from channel strategy to landing pages to attribution




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and run paid acquisition across Google, LinkedIn, and other B2B channels

 - Own landing page strategy and production for campaigns and paid traffic, including ABM

 - Drive SEO and AEO (answer engine optimization) as AI search becomes a core lead source

 - Run growth experiments across channels to find what scales

 - Own attribution, analytics, and pipeline reporting for marketing-sourced leads

 - Partner with PMM on campaign messaging and with sales on lead quality and handoff

 - Support field marketing with digital amplification (conferences, partner campaigns, events)




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years in demand gen or growth marketing at B2B SaaS, infra, or developer tools companies

 - Proven track record building paid acquisition programs from scratch, ideally for high-ACV enterprise sales

 - Strong paid ads chops across Google, LinkedIn, and emerging channels

 - Data-driven. Comfortable with attribution models, funnel analysis, and pipeline reporting.

 - Landing page and conversion optimization experience

 - Understanding of SEO and AEO; experience with tools like Profound is a plus

 - Comfortable running experiments fast and killing what doesn't work




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with high-ACV, technical enterprise sales motions (six figure deals, long sales cycles)

 - AI-native, uses Claude




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and certifications in AI, enterprise software, and customer success.

 - Top-tier equipment — your choice of laptop and accessories to create your ideal work environment.

 - Health benefits — comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage.

 - Customer interaction opportunities — work directly with innovative companies and see the immediate impact of your work.



Not quite the right fit? Reach out to careers@runlayer.com careers@anysource.com with details about yo

source_jobs.title: Regional Sales Director, East
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Regional Sales Director for the East, you'll lead and grow Runlayer's sales motion across the Eastern US. You'll take over an existing team of AEs and book of business from day one, then hire and build your own team from there. Reporting to the VP of GTM, you'll be hands-on as both a coach and a closer; running a disciplined operating rhythm, developing rising talent, and winning complex, multi-stakeholder deals with security and engineering buyers. This is an early-stage, build-from-the-ground-up role for someone who's done it before and wants to do it again.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Build the region that proves how AI-first enterprises buy security and governance.

 - Excellence: Lead a team of A-players and partner closely with field engineering and leadership on every deal.

 - Ownership: Start with an existing book and team, and own how the East scales from day one.
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing team of AEs, and build a culture where great people want to stay.

 - Take over an existing team and book of business, then hire and build out your own team, keeping a strong bench in play.

 - Own regional go-to-market strategy: territory planning, pipeline health, multi-threaded executive alignment, and close plans.

 - Own the operating cadence: forecasting, pipeline inspection, deal reviews, and weekly coaching.

 - Win complex, multi-stakeholder deals by aligning field engineering, product, and partnerships to move them forward.

 - Build relationships with technical buyers (security and engineering leaders) and economic buyers (VPs and C-suite).

 - Partner with marketing on targeted programs and field events like executive dinners, and work with customers on renewal and expansion.

 - Translate what you hear in the field into concrete input on product, pricing, and packaging.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Directly managed AEs and have a track record of hitting team targets.

 - You've personally closed at the highest level, and can still get in the room and move a deal.

 - Startup sales leadership experience, ideally building an early-stage region from zero; a builder mindset, not just a big-company operator.

 - Genuine early-stage appetite: you want to build something from the ground up again.

 - Command of enterprise sales methodology with rigor around pipeline hygiene, coverage, and forecast accuracy.

 - Comfortable selling into technical audiences: you earn the trust of security and engineering buyers.

 - A coaching-first leadership style and strong judgment on talent and culture fit.

 - You set a high bar and a hard pace, without losing the fun that makes people want to stay.

 - Enterprise SaaS and AI experience preferred; we're industry-agnostic, but that combo is ideal.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience selling complex platforms in security, infrastructure,

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Forward-Deployed Engineer, you'll own the technical side of customer GTM at Runlayer. You'll run POCs, troubleshoot integrations, and work closely with enterprise customers to get them live.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: if a POC ships, you made it happen. You're directly responsible for turning prospects into customers.

 - Excellence: work alongside experienced engineers at the intersection of enterprise sales and deep technical work.

 - Ownership: own customer outcomes end-to-end, from first technical deep dive through full deployment.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own POC outcomes for enterprise customers.

 - Run pre-sales technical deep dives, demos, and architecture discussions.

 - Troubleshoot integrations across customer environments (identity, security, networking).

 - Build custom configurations and one-off solutions when the standard path doesn't fit.

 - Run technical onboarding sessions and adoption workshops.

 - Be the technical expert in the room during sales cycles, handling stump questions from senior engineers.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of FDE / SA / SE experience with a CS background

 - Background in solutions engineering, pre-sales, or consulting with real technical depth. You can write and debug production code independently, not just run demos.

 - Hands-on experience building AI agents. You can talk through architecture, memory, and tool design.

 - Depth in at least one area relevant to our space: identity and auth, MCP, agent frameworks, security, deployment, networking, observability, etc

 - AI-native workflow. You use AI to build, not just to prompt.

 - Executive presence. You can walk into a room of senior technical executives and hold the conversation.

 - High agency. You solve problems with high ownership and the drive to make sure a solution is delivered.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Prior solutions, pre-sales, or forward-deployed role at a high-growth B2B startup.

 - Open-source contributions or content in agent, identity, or policy spaces




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and certifications in AI, enterprise software, and customer success.

 - Top-tier equipment — your choice of laptop and accessories to create your ideal work environment.

 - Health benefits — comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage.

 - Customer interaction opportunities — work directly with innovative companies and see the immediate impact of your work.



Not quite the right fit? Reach out to careers@runlayer.com careers@anysource.com w

source_jobs.title: Head of Channel Sales
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Head of Channel Sales, you'll build a new revenue engine for Runlayer through the cybersecurity reseller, value-added reseller, and partner ecosystem. You'll own the channel motion end-to-end, from initial relationship development through commercial structure, enablement, and revenue realization.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: You will open and scale an entirely new line of business for Runlayer. The channel is where some of the most strategic enterprise security buyers transact, and you are the person who unlocks it.

 - Excellence: You'll work directly with the VP of Go-to-Market, the CEO, and the Account Executive team to position Runlayer with the partners who already have deep relationships across the Fortune 500.

 - Ownership: You'll bring an established network of partner relationships, map the most influential resellers and VARs in the security ecosystem, structure the commercial and operational frameworks that make partnerships work, and represent Runlayer as the senior point of contact across the channel community.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build the channel from the ground up. Define Runlayer's channel strategy, identify the partners that matter most, and stand up the relationships, programs, and processes that will scale.

 - Activate your network on day one. Use existing relationships across the cybersecurity reseller and VAR community to accelerate adoption of Runlayer and shorten the time to first revenue.

 - Map and prioritize the ecosystem. Identify which resellers, VARs, and partner organizations align with Runlayer's enterprise customer base and prioritize where to invest first.

 - Own the commercial relationship. Lead negotiations, structure partner agreements, and manage the full commercial lifecycle with each partner.

 - Educate partners on Runlayer. Equip partner-side sellers, solutions engineers, and account teams with the knowledge they need to position Runlayer effectively.

 - Drive partner-sourced revenue. Own the channel pipeline and revenue target, and report against it.

 - Facilitate introductions and co-selling motions. Bridge partner relationships into Runlayer's direct sales team to drive joint enterprise opportunities.

 - Partner cross-functionally. Work with Go-to-Market, Marketing, Product, and Operations to ensure partners are supported through every stage of the customer lifecycle.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Established channel experience. Seven or more years of relevant professional experience, with significant time spent building or leading channel and partnership functions at a high-growth security or enterprise SaaS company.

 - Strong, active relationships. A deep and current network of relationships across the leading cybersecurity reseller and VAR community. References from partner-side contacts at top resellers carry significant weight.

 - Track record of building channel mo

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff - Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Product Engineer to work directly with our customers, translating problems into new product. You'll sit on the Product team, writing production code while working with customers. The goal: deeply understand customer needs and help inform our roadmap.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Your work with customers directly shapes product direction. What you learn on Monday could be in the roadmap by Friday.

 - Excellence: Work alongside top-tier engineers and directly with enterprise customers, learning from both technical challenges and business needs.

 - Ownership: Own the full loop from customer discovery through product delivery and feedback.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Work directly with enterprise customers to understand their needs. You'll conduct discovery sessions, demos, and ongoing collaboration to learn how they're using the product and what's not working.

 - Shape our product roadmap. You'll translate customer patterns and pain points into product requirements, helping prioritize what we build next.

 - Build solutions that validate product direction. You'll prototype and ship code that tests hypotheses about what customers need, whether that's custom integrations, new features, or workflow improvements.

 - Present to customers and show them how to use the product. You're comfortable demoing, training, and gathering feedback in real-time.

 - Close the feedback loop. You'll track how customers use what you've built and iterate based on real-world usage.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Senior engineering expertise. 4+ years of software engineering experience with proficiency in Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, and modern development practices.

 - Customer obsession. You genuinely enjoy working with customers, understanding their problems, and iterating until you've solved them. You're comfortable presenting and demoing.

 - Product instincts. You see patterns across customer conversations and know which problems are worth solving at the platform level vs. one-off solutions.

 - API and integration experience. Deep experience building integrations with various APIs, databases, and enterprise systems.

 - AI and LLM familiarity. Experience working with AI/ML systems and deep expertise with AI-assisted development workflows.

 - Communication skills. Excellent written and verbal communication, able to translate between customer needs and engineering requirements.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - MCP or agent framework experience. Direct experience with Model Context Protocol, FastMCP, Vercel AI SDK, Agents SDK, LangGraph, or similar AI agent frameworks.

 - Enterprise software experience. Background working with enterprise systems like Salesforce, ServiceNow, Jira, Slack, or similar platforms.




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise c

source_jobs.title: Enablement Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Enablement Lead, you'll own how new Runners get up to speed: the design, the build, and the execution of onboarding and enablement from end to end. You'll start where the need is sharpest, our scaling go-to-market team, with a mandate that grows across the company from there. Your job is to compress ramp time, and to do it the Runlayer way.

Here's what we mean. The old playbook is slide decks, thirty Zoom walkthroughs, and a feedback survey. We're not doing that. Our onboarding puts new hires inside the product on day one: they use it, feel where it breaks, and learn why we built what we built before anyone hands them a talk track. We've already built the v1 of this program, including an AI tutor agent that drills new hires, simulates customer objections, and reports on their progress. You'll take it over, run it, and make it world-class.




WHY YOU’LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Every hire we make ramps through your program. As we scale GTM, the speed at which a new AE, SDR, or SE becomes productive is the speed at which the company grows.

 - Excellence: You'll work directly with our VP of Go-to-Market and Head of Operations, with a mandate to build the most elite onboarding program in enterprise software.

 - Ownership: This is a build role. You own the content, the calendar, the certification gates, the AI agents that power the program, and the results.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own onboarding end to end. Design, build, and run the program that takes a new hire from signed offer to certified and selling. You own ramp time as a number and are accountable for moving it down.

 - Evolve our onboarding program. We've taken the first cut: a structured first week built around hands-on labs, certification gates, and real product use. You'll pressure-test it, run it with every cohort, and rebuild it as the product and team grow.

 - Build AI-native enablement. Conceive and design interactive sessions and voice agents that simulate customer calls, test product knowledge, and pressure-test objection handling. You won't need to write the code yourself, but you'll shape what these agents do, calibrate them against real cohorts, and spot the gaps that keep them from teaching well.

 - Teach the product by feel, not by slides. New Runners should sell from experience: they've deployed the platform, broken it, governed it, and built with it. You'll keep that principle at the core of everything you ship.

 - Enable the field beyond week one. Ongoing readiness as we launch new products, enter new segments, and refine positioning. Onboarding is the start; keeping the team sharp is the job.

 - Grow the scope. GTM is the starting point, not the ceiling. Parts of the program already double as the company-wide layer for every new Runner, and as we scale, this role is positioned to own enablement across the company.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of e

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff - Security
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Looking for a security engineer, to join our small founding team, you'll build the security scanning and detection products that protect enterprise AI. You own the Runlayer Watch products (static and dynamic scanning), shadow detection of unregistered agents and servers, and AppSec for the platform itself.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Build the security layer for the AI agent infrastructure category, directly shaping how enterprises adopt AI safely

 - Excellence: Work alongside founders from Zapier's AI team and a team of senior engineers from top cyber backgrounds

 - Ownership: Own detection products end-to-end, from threat modeling through shipped features




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and improve Watch products: static and dynamic scanning for MCP servers, skills, plugins, and agent behavior detection on endpoints

 - Develop shadow detection: identify unregistered MCP servers, skills, plugins, and agents running outside governance across the enterprise

 - Own AppSec for the platform: penetration testing, vulnerability management, dependency scanning, and security hardening of the control plane

 - Build automated version scanning: CI/CD-integrated security checks that run on each new MCP server version, skill update, or plugin release

 - Extend detection coverage to CLI agents (Codex, OpenCode) and browser-based agents




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 8+ years in security engineering with deep experience in application security, security tooling development, or endpoint detection

 - Builder, not operator. You've created scanning or detection systems: parsers, rule engines, analysis pipelines.

 - Experience with shadow IT detection, asset discovery, or endpoint monitoring in enterprise environments

 - Strong Python skills (our scanning pipeline and platform backend are Python/FastAPI)

 - Understanding of API and gateway attack patterns: SSRF, token theft, injection, supply-chain attacks

 - Awareness of emerging AI/LLM security threats: prompt injection, tool poisoning, jailbreaking, indirect prompt injection through tool responses




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with MCP, AI agents, or LLM security specifically

 - Background in building commercial security products (not just internal tooling)

 - Network in enterprise security (SVCI, Israeli security community, etc.)




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and certifications in AI, enterprise software, and customer success.

 - Top-tier equipment — your choice of laptop and accessories to create your ideal wor

We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Site Reliability Engineer, you'll own the reliability, performance, and scalability of Runlayer's infrastructure as we grow to serve enterprise customers across cloud and on-prem environments.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Build the infrastructure foundation for the enterprise MCP platform, directly enabling AI adoption at scale

 - Excellence: Work closely with founders and a small, senior engineering team shipping fast in a high-growth environment

 - Ownership: Own reliability end-to-end, from database performance to incident response to CI/CD pipelines




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own reliability and performance of our cloud infrastructure across AWS (ECS, Aurora, CloudWatch) and GCP

 - Manage and optimize Kubernetes clusters and container orchestration

 - Drive database reliability engineering, including performance tuning and scaling

 - Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for rapid, safe deployments

 - Run incident response and on-call rotations

 - Partner with product engineers to design scalable, resilient systems




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong AWS experience, particularly ECS, Aurora, and CloudWatch

 - GCP experience as we expand cross-cloud

 - Kubernetes and container orchestration expertise

 - DBRE experience with database performance tuning

 - CI/CD pipeline ownership and incident response experience

 - Background at a B2B SaaS company serving enterprise customers, ideally in infrastructure




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience deploying and supporting on-prem or hybrid environments

 - Python backend familiarity (our platform is Python-based)

 - Experience at an early-stage or high-growth company




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and certifications in AI, enterprise software, and customer success.

 - Top-tier equipment — your choice of laptop and accessories to create your ideal work environment.

 - Health benefits — comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage.

 - Customer interaction opportunities — work directly with innovative companies and see the immediate impact of your work.



Not quite the right fit? Reach out to careers@runlayer.com careers@anysource.com with details about your experience and interests.

We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Creative Director, you'll own video, motion, and visual storytelling across Runlayer. You'll create launch videos, product explainers, and demo content while overseeing case study production with external vendors. This is a hands-on role: you push pixels, not just strategy.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Define the visual language of the enterprise AI control plane at a critical inflection point

 - Excellence: Work alongside our renowned product designer from Square and Meta Reels, and founders who care deeply about craft

 - Ownership: Own all video and motion output, from quick explainers to polished customer stories




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Create launch videos, product demos, and narrative explainer videos (think Servo-style, not high production live action)

 - Design and produce motion graphics for product releases, features, and campaigns

 - Oversee external vendors on customer case studies and testimonials

 - Push pixels on web: landing pages, campaign assets, visual identity extensions

 - Establish the motion and video language that scales as we build out the marketing team




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong motion design and video production skills. You can concept, design, and ship a launch video yourself.

 - Interaction design sensibility: you understand product and can translate features into compelling visual stories

 - Experience with demo videos, explainer content, and product storytelling (not just brand spots)

 - Proficiency in motion tools (After Effects, etc.) and design tools (Figma)

 - Ability to direct external video crews and vendors for higher-production content

 - Taste. You know the difference between forgettable B2B video and content that actually lands.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Background in tech, developer tools, or enterprise SaaS

 - Experience at a high-craft consumer brand (Nike, Apple, etc.) or design-forward startup

 - Web design chops in addition to motion




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and certifications in AI, enterprise software, and customer success.

 - Top-tier equipment — your choice of laptop and accessories to create your ideal work environment.

 - Health benefits — comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage.

 - Customer interaction opportunities — work directly with innovative companies and see the immediate impact of your work.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring sales hires to drive new business at Runlayer. Your mission is simple but ambitious: get us in the room with the right people at the right time. You’ll source meetings, build relationships, and close early deals that shape how enterprises adopt AI.




WHY YOU’LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: You’ll be directly responsible for putting Runlayer in front of the world’s best companies, creating opportunities that define our trajectory in this market.

 - Excellence: Work shoulder-to-shoulder with our founders (ex-Zapier AI leadership) and top-tier advisors. You’ll learn enterprise AI from the people who built it at scale.

 - Ownership: As our first sales hire, you’ll run the outbound motion end-to-end, iterating on what works.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Source and qualify meetings with senior leaders at Fortune 500 and high-growth enterprises. You’ll build pipeline through outbound prospecting, events, and creative hustle.

 - Run early-stage deals. Manage the full sales cycle from first touch to signed contract, collaborating with the founders and engineers to close.

 - Craft and refine our pitch. You’ll quickly learn what resonates with prospects and help evolve our story in real time.

 - Build a repeatable motion. Document and systematize outreach strategies, helping lay the foundation for a world-class go-to-market engine.

 - Be the face of Runlayer. Represent us at events, conferences, and customer meetings in NYC, SF and beyond.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Hunter mentality. You thrive on outbound prospecting and love the chase of getting into new accounts.

 - Early-career sales experience. 4+ years of experience in outbound sales, BDR/SDR, or AE roles at high-growth SaaS or infra companies.

 - Proven track record. Consistent quota overachievement with recognition such as President’s Club or equivalent top-performer awards.

 - Fearless communicator. Excellent written and verbal communication skills—you can craft crisp emails and deliver compelling pitches.

 - Hustle and persistence. Comfortable sending the 5th follow-up, picking up the phone, and finding a way to make contact.

 - Learning mindset. You’re hungry to understand enterprise AI and willing to dive deep into both technical and business conversations.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience selling developer tools, infrastructure, or security products.

 - Existing network in enterprise IT, security, or AI at enterprises.

 - Track record of closing six-figure deals at an early stage.

 - Expert-level in using AI tools for prospecting and outreach.




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid pa

Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Founding PM for Agents, you'll own where agents and skills get built, shipped, and run at scale, the growth engine of our customers. That means the fastest path from an idea to a working agent or skill, for engineers and non-technical builders alike, plus everything that happens once it's built: agents running on schedules and triggers, calling each other, governed by Runlayer underneath, and getting better over time.

You'll work directly with the CPO, owning the roadmap from discovery through shipped product.



WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Build the tools that turn one builder into a team, and a team into an org running on agents.

 - Excellence: Work closely with founders who helped establish MCP and ship fast.

 - Ownership: Own the builder and the runtime end to end, from discovery through shipped product.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the builder: natural language to working agent, self improvement, versioning, and sharing.

 - Own the runtime: event-based triggers, scheduling, model routing, and agent delegation.

 - Drive growth: Slack Agents, time-to-first-working-agent, and the self-serve path that turns one builder into a team into an org.

 - Define how product telemetry makes agents better over time.

 - Serve both audiences: the engineer who wants SDK depth and the non-technical builder who wants natural language. Run discovery directly with both.

 - Make decisions, prioritize, and ship with engineers weekly.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 8+ years in product management, including 3+ on developer tools, platform, or builder/automation products.

 - Founder or early-PM experience; you've built product processes from zero.

 - Shipped 0→1 builder or automation surfaces, with a grasp of PLG and bottoms-up growth: activation, time-to-value, and sharing loops.

 - Technical depth: you read code and understand API/SDK design, agent frameworks, and how agents call tools.

 - Customer obsession: you run your own discovery calls and can name five customers from your last role by first name.

 - Strong writing; you make your case in clear PRDs, not slide decks.

 - AI-native: you build with agents daily and have real opinions on how they should be created, orchestrated, and improved.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Hands-on building of MCP, skills, and how agents call tools and each other in production.

 - Experience with workflow or agent orchestration, triggers, or scheduling systems.

 - A track record turning a builder product into a growth engine (self-serve, templates,




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional devel

Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Founding PM for the Control Plane, you'll own one governance plane for the full agent lifecycle: every MCP, skill, plugin, and agent, governed and observable across every surface an agent can touch. Today agents sprawl across every platform with little oversight; you'll build the one layer that sits between their agentic harnesses and the hundreds of SaaS apps their agents reach.

You'll work directly with the CPO, owning the roadmap from discovery through shipped product.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Own the control plane that lets enterprises adopt AI agents without losing control.

 - Excellence: Work closely with founders who helped establish MCP and ship fast.

 - Ownership: Own the full control-plane surface end to end, from discovery through shipped product.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the control-plane surface: discovery, visibility, deep session, real-time controls, and agent IAM.

 - Run discovery with security and IT buyers and the developers and agents who hit the proxy, turning signals into a ranked roadmap.

 - Make decisions, prioritize the roadmap, and ship with engineers weekly.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 8+ years in product management, including 3+ on security, infrastructure, or identity/IAM products.

 - Founder or early-PM experience; you've built product processes from zero.

 - Fluency with the buyer/user split: the CISO/IT team who buys governance and the developer or agent who lives under it.

 - Technical depth: you read code and reason about proxies, policy engines, OAuth/identity, and audit pipelines.

 - Customer obsession: you run your own discovery calls and can name five customers from your last role by first name.

 - Strong writing; you make your case in clear PRDs, not slide decks.

 - AI-native, with real opinions on how agents and tools should be governed.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Hands-on building of MCP and how agents call tools in production.

 - Background in detection and response, DLP/PII/PHI, secrets/vault, or compliance-driven enterprise sales (SOC 2, HIPAA, NIST).

 - Shipped observability or policy products where winning meant covering more, in more detail, than anyone else.




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and certifications in AI, enterprise software, and customer success.

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff - Identity
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Identity engineer, you'll own the authentication and authorization layer that secures every AI-to-enterprise connection on Runlayer, from the OAuth broker behind our enterprise integrations to how AI agents prove who they are and get auditable access. You'll work directly with customers and help shape the MCP Auth spec that defines how agent identity works industry-wide.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Own the identity and authentication layer that secures every AI-to-enterprise connection on our platform.

 - Excellence: Work alongside engineers who've shipped AI systems at scale.

 - Ownership: Shape how MCP & AI agent authentication works, from spec-level decisions to production code.
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Architect and implement authentication and authorization systems for MCP servers (OAuth 2.0, Dynamic Client Registration, token management).

 - Build and extend our OAuth broker that handles enterprise identity integrations across dozens of vendors.

 - Design identity propagation for AI agents, ensuring secure, auditable access to enterprise systems.

 - Integrate with enterprise identity providers (Okta, WorkOS, Azure AD) and SCIM systems.

 - Define fine-grained access control policies for MCP tools and resources.

 - Collaborate directly with customers like Gusto and Rippling to solve real-world identity challenges.

 - Contribute to the MCP Auth spec and help define how agent identity works industry-wide.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience with significant focus on identity, authentication, or authorization systems.

 - Deep experience with OAuth 2.0/OIDC, including DCR, token exchange, and audience restriction.

 - Background building or integrating with enterprise identity systems (Okta, WorkOS, Auth0, AWS IAM, GCP IAM).

 - Strong fundamentals in distributed systems and API security.

 - Experience with Python and TypeScript (our stack is Python/FastAPI backend, TypeScript/React frontend).

 - Comfortable working directly with enterprise customers to understand and solve their security requirements.

 - Heavy AI user who leverages tools like Claude Code or Cursor to multiply output.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with Kubernetes-native authorization patterns or service mesh security.

 - Background in ML security (differential privacy, LLM security research).

 - Prior work on identity for multi-tenant SaaS platforms.

 - Familiarity with the MCP specification.




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and c

We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our first Field Marketing Manager you'll own Runlayer's presence at every event, from major conferences to intimate dinners. You'll run the field motion end to end: choosing which conferences to sponsor, allocating budget, partnering with sales and BDRs to drive attendance, and closing the loop on post-event follow-up and pipeline. This is a build-from-scratch role, working directly with the VP of Marketing and the sales team.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Put Runlayer in front of the security and engineering leaders who decide how enterprises adopt AI.

 - Excellence: Work closely with founders and a marketing team scaling from founder-led to a real function.

 - Ownership: Own the entire field motion from scratch, from conference selection to sourced pipeline.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the field calendar end to end: conference selection, budget allocation, sponsorship negotiation, and deciding what to skip.

 - Run intimate events: exec dinners and CISO roundtables, from invite list to follow-up.

 - Partner with AEs and BDRs: target account lists, pre-event outreach, on-site meeting orchestration, and post-event handoffs.

 - Close the loop on pipeline: follow-up sequences, sourced and influenced attribution, and reporting field ROI to leadership.

 - Execute on-site: booth presence, event collateral, and swag, representing Runlayer to security and engineering buyers.

 - Build the field motion from scratch alongside the VP of Marketing and the sales team.

 - This role will require up to 40% travel for events and customer engagements.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3-6 years in field marketing.

 - Extremely organized, with a track record juggling many events and details at once.

 - A track record partnering closely with sales, measured on pipeline rather than event logistics.

 - Ecosystem instinct: a real opinion on where AI infrastructure and security buyers actually show up.

 - Actively uses AI tools to multiply output, in the real marketing workflow, not occasionally.

 - A founding mindset: you want to build from scratch, not inherit an established playbook.




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - AI-native and infra-literate: you can talk to engineers and understand what the product actually does.

 - Experience marketing a technical or security product to engineering and security buyers.

 - Familiarity with the MCP and AI agents space.




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and certifications in AI, enterprise software, and customer success.

 - Top-tier equipment — you

source_jobs.title: Developer Relations Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RUNLAYER

AI is transforming how every company operates, but most enterprises are stuck. They want to move fast with AI Agents, tools, and workflows, but they can't do it safely. We're fixing that.



Our team built AI Actions for OpenAI, shipped Zapier Agents to millions of users, and launched the first remote MCP server with Anthropic. We helped establish the protocol, and now we're building the platform enterprises need to actually put MCP to work.



Runlayer is one platform for MCPs, Skills, and Agents: purpose-built security, fine-grained governance, and complete observability so organizations can go all-in on AI across the entire company without the risk. We just raised a $30M Series A led by Felicis, with participation from Khosla Ventures, bringing our total raised to $42M. Already trusted by Gusto, Instacart, Opendoor, dbt Labs, Cursor, and Decagon.



We're a team of 35, mostly engineers, shipping fast. Agent operators on Runlayer grew 5x in four weeks while human operators stayed flat. The shift to agentic work is already showing up in our own usage.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Developer Relations Lead, you'll own Runlayer's physical presence in the developer and enterprise AI communities. You'll manage our sponsorships and booth presence at major conferences while building a community that our customers want to be part of.




WHY YOU'LL THRIVE HERE

 - Impact: Own how Runlayer shows up at every conference that matters in the AI and enterprise infrastructure space

 - Excellence: Work with a team that treats end user love & events as a core GTM motion

 - Ownership: Own conference strategy, DevRel programming, and community presence end-to-end




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own major conference presence: AWS re:Invent, AI Engineer World's Fair, MCP Dev Summit, RSA, and others

 - Run DevRel events: meetups, hackathons, dinners, and technical workshops for MCP developers and platform engineers

 - Manage booth strategy, speaking slots, side events, and customer dinners at conferences

 - Build and nurture the technical community around Runlayer and the MCP ecosystem

 - Create technical content and demos that can travel to events

 - Partner with engineering and product to bring technical credibility to every event

 - Drive partner co-marketing activations at events (Cursor, 1Password, Anthropic, etc.)




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years in developer relations, technical community, or technical events at B2B SaaS, infra, or AI companies

 - Technical background. You can hold your own in a room with senior engineers and have written code at some point in your career.

 - Proven experience running major conference sponsorships and booth operations end-to-end

 - Strong taste for what makes events memorable vs forgettable (Vercel, Stripe, Linear as reference points)

 - Comfortable on stage and in front of technical audiences; able to deliver demos and talks when needed

 - Network in the developer, AI, or infrastructure communities




BONUS QUALIFICATIONS

 - Direct experience with MCP, AI agents, or LLM tooling

 - Background as an engineer before moving into DevRel

 - Track record of building technical communities from scratch

 - Experience running hackathons or technical workshops




WHAT WE OFFER

We provide a competitive package designed to attract and retain top talent who can work effectively with enterprise customers.

 - Competitive salary and equity — compensation that reflects your expertise and customer-facing responsibilities.

 - Paid time off — paid vacation, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave.

 - Professional development — budget for conferences, courses, and certifications in AI, enterprise software, and customer success.

 - Top-tier equipment — your choice of laptop and accessories to create your ideal work environment.

 - Health benefits — comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage.

 - Customer interaction opportunities — work directly with innovative companies and see the im

notes: High tech role ratio, frequent postings, and lack of explicit sponsorship mention suggest a startup with high engineering demand.
